own_review_points: Writability boolean returned by write ops is computed but not yet consumed; risk of stale/racy backpressure if callers adopt it prematurely
Switch from per-stream byte-tracking (writeWindow/writeOverflow removed) to message-count water marks changes backpressure semantics: one large buffer == many small ones
writeQueueFull() now delegates to conn.writeQueueFull() where it previously returned false — behavioral change for stream consumers
MPSC queue: hook2 invoked from producer thread on highWaterMark while overflow/writeQueueFull are documented consumer-only — thread-context must be clearly documented
drainLoop/WIP increment correctness around highWaterMark return value (the human-ref point at OutboundWriteQueue:221)
notes: v8 shows real domain understanding of the MPSC/backpressure design and the backpressure-semantics-change point is substantive and likely valid. But the queue internals it cites (lines 103-105, 221-229, sendFile:482) are not in the truncated diff, so those points are unverifiable rather than confirmed. It misses the specific drainLoop return-value correctness issue the human reviewer raised at line 221.
